text:
It’s not just gold, silver, and bronze medals that athletes are receiving on the podiums at the2024 Paris Olympics. With the action at the summer Games now well underway, more than 30 events have already been decided withTeam GB winning their first gold on Monday in the team equestrian.

The medals have been specially designed, with a hexagonal piece of the Eiffel Tower set into the mementoes.

The iron fragments were removed from the tower and preserved during renovations before the turn of the century, and have now been used to add a historic element to the medals.

Pick up your ultimate guide to the Olympics! Going for Gold provides the day-by-day rundown of what's on and when. Clickhereto buy.

A toy version of the Paris 2024 official mascot, the red Olympic Phryge, is also being handed out. And in addition, viewers have noted that top-three finishers have been receiving small brown boxes, prompting debate over what was actually in them.

It’s been revealed that the boxes, which are around 40 centimetres long, all contain a rolled-up copy of the official Paris 2024 Olympic poster. Designed and drawn by famous Parisian illustrator Ugo Gattoni, the poster is nothing if not unique.

The image depicts a cartoon version of Paris with the words “XXXIII Olympiade,” referencing that France is hosting the 33rd edition of the Summer Games. The River Seine and red Eiffel Tower also feature on the poster.



Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

A diver has also been drawn into the bottom right corner, appearing to be watching on as the Olympic spectacle unfolds. The posters were unveiled in March and went on show for a week at the Musée d'Orsay.

“It's a new, key moment in the Paris 2024 story," said Paris 2024 president Tony Estanguet at the event. "We've tried to be different and imagine posters that look like us, posters that go beyond a mere logo."

Gattoni reportedly spent more than 2,000 hours creating the poster. He is also renowned for writing the book Bicycle in 2012, the year of the London Olympics, which included images of a mass cycle race taking place in the English capital.

Overall, 329 medal events will take place during the Olympic Games. The ceremonies are taking place at a variety of competition venues, with organisers maintaining the tradition of raising the national flag of each medal winner.

Team GB’s first medal came early on Saturday morning after Yasmin Harper and Scarlett Mew Jensen claimed diving bronze. The total is now up to six - three silver, two bronze and one gold.

title: What is in the cardboard boxes handed to Olympic medallists on podiums? | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Fraser Watson
description: Television viewers have noticed that athletes have been getting a bonus gift at the medal ceremonies in Paris.
